raw:
{
  "judge_id": "your_judge_id",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 3,
    "R1.4": 2,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 3,
    "RX.4": 3,
    "RX.5": 1
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The review identifies a conceptual issue (the overstated 'learned router') and proposes a reframing (a simple confidence-thresholded policy). However, it doesn't fully analyze the drift from the original framing or propose a solution that survives prior work contrast.",
    "R1.4": "The review mentions one prior work (cloud-only inference) but doesn't map against multiple works or articulate sharp differences.",
    "RX.1": "The review expresses uncertainty about the effectiveness of future work (co-occurrence priors) and acknowledges methodological gaps.",
    "RX.3": "The review mostly stays within scope, focusing on the described routing method and deployment latency. However, it points out a lack of bandwidth constraints validation.",
    "RX.4": "The review doesn't identify any new errors or harmful content but highlights potential regressions (lack of baselines, unclear latency methodology).",
    "RX.5": "The review provides some traceability by pointing out missing elements and suggesting improvements, but it doesn't explicitly annotate every non-cosmetic change."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.8,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.8,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.6
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The review identifies a conceptual issue (the overstated 'learned router') and proposes a reframing (a simple confidence-thresholded policy). However, it doesn't fully analyze the drift from the original framing or propose a solution that survives prior work contrast.
rationale.R1.4: The review mentions one prior work (cloud-only inference) but doesn't map against multiple works or articulate sharp differences.
rationale.RX.1: The review expresses uncertainty about the effectiveness of future work (co-occurrence priors) and acknowledges methodological gaps.
rationale.RX.3: The review mostly stays within scope, focusing on the described routing method and deployment latency. However, it points out a lack of bandwidth constraints validation.
rationale.RX.4: The review doesn't identify any new errors or harmful content but highlights potential regressions (lack of baselines, unclear latency methodology).
rationale.RX.5: The review provides some traceability by pointing out missing elements and suggesting improvements, but it doesn't explicitly annotate every non-cosmetic change.
